aim.intro: The Request for Tender (RFT) is for the this procurement to Council for the service of the procurement, installation, and integration of the specified works.

general: All work must comply with Australian Standards, NSW WHS regulations, and Council requirements.
A site visit is mandatory before tender submission.
Minimise disruption to operations; schedule works outside peak hours where possible.

testing_commissioning: 10.1 General
Provide all equipment, labour, materials, power, consumables, etc. to conduct testing and
commissioning. The Contractor shall carry out his own tests to verify operation and performance
before requesting Council to attend inspections. Submit fully detailed Test Log sheets for inspection
prior to any testing taking place. The Test Log sheets shall be A4 in size and present a clear and
precise record of all tests to be carried out.
10.2 Testing
The following tests and checks shall be carried out by the Contractor:
1. Visual inspection of pipework and joints for leaks.
2. Water-test drains and overflow piping for leaks and for unobstructed drainage.
3. Check all power cabling terminations and megger test each circuit.
4. Verify integrity of all controls cabling to ensure electrical continuity and freedom from shorts and
grounds.
5. Sequence checks of all specified control functions and cycles, such as Start, Stop, Chiller Staging,
Cooling and Heating Cycles, etc.
6. Water flow rates shall be balanced to +5% / -0% of those scheduled. Measure and record Chilled
Water flow rates, compare with scheduled design water flows, and submit.
Make all necessary adjustments and record all temperature set points and differentials on the Test
Log sheets.
BLCS Main Pool Hall Heating & Ventilation - AHU 1 & 3 Refurbishments                                                                     Page 19 of 20
maintenance_warranty: 12-month defects liability, scheduled servicing, and emergency maintenance.
The Installation Contractor is responsible for carrying out all preventative maintenance and servicing
for the duration of the Defects Liability Period.
The mechanical contractor shall allow in the tender for maintenance and servicing from date of
practical completion to the end of the maintenance and defects liability period, to comply with
requirements of AS 3666, Local Authority and any other statutory body having jurisdiction.
Maintenance, in addition to rectification of faults, emergency service and carrying out of capacity
tests as required, shall also include a minimum of 12 regular monthly visits for routine maintenance.

invoicing: All council invoices are paid on 30-day terms.
Invoices must include detailed breakdown of labour, materials, and charges.

weekly: Work completed; issues/mitigations; next period tasks.
final: System performance results; commissioning reports; warranty documents; maintenance schedule.

performance_monitoring.note: The Service Provider will meet with Council as required to monitor performance.

purpose_rft: Council seeks this procurement for procurement, installation and integration with a service/repair term.

probity_and_accountability.probity: Probity is an integral element of tendering and contracting. Objectives: value for money; accountability; competitive neutrality; public confidence; defensibility of decisions. Principles: open competition; transparency; conflict identification/management; accountability; performance monitoring.
probity_and_accountability.confidentiality_and_conflict: All committee members/advisers sign Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest declarations. Declare relationships and avoid gifts/benefits. Replace conflicted members where necessary.
probity_and_accountability.security_and_confidentiality: Maintain confidentiality and secure handling of submissions and evaluation materials. Store electronic records in controlled directories; physical documents in secure storage.
probity_and_accountability.advertising_rft: Advertised on Council website and WSROC NSW e-tendering.
probity_and_accountability.receipt_of_tenders: Tenders must be lodged electronically at the Tendering website by the closing time.
probity_and_accountability.late_tenders: Late tenders handled per RFT provisions; exceptions require satisfactory evidence.
probity_and_accountability.requests_for_clarification: Clarifications are coordinated by the Authorised Contact Officer and recorded in writing. No new or additional information may be introduced.

tender_evaluation.methodology_text: The Evaluation Committee, in accordance with the methodology specified in the RFT and reproduced here, will evaluate all tenders.

past_performance_referee_scale.full: Full achievement ... no errors, weaknesses or omissions.
Reasonable achievement ... some errors that can be corrected.
Minimal achievement ... errors possible to correct.

methodology_scale.requirement: Practical and reliable; minor issues only.

resources_scale.requirement: Lack of resources; cannot perform without hiring.

referee_questions: WHS plan & PPE compliance; incident history?
Quality plan incl. ITPs; records/invoices on time?

financial_scale.full: Marginal cashflow; turnover slightly less than spend.

verification_scale.requirement: Well-documented QMS, third-party accredited, excellent QMP & ITPs.
QMS; third-party accredited; very good QMP & ITPs.
QMS with good examples of QMP & ITPs.
Accredited QMS; fair examples of plans.
Comprehensive WHS system & manual; excellent evidence.
Good WHS system & manual; very good evidence.
Reasonably good WHS system & manual; good evidence.
Comprehensive EMS; excellent policies/practices; excellent EMP.
Good EMS; very good policies/practices; very good EMP.
Reasonably good EMS; good policies/practices; good EMP.

evidence_items: Wages and superannuation in accordance with legislation
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander employment policies & implementation

pricing.pricing_model_text:
Pricing for tender evaluation will be normalised; the lowest fee achieves the maximum score (equal to the price weighting). State Government method:
Ps = 200 – (Pc / Pav × 100)
Pn = Ps / 200
Pw = Pn × [Price Weight]
Pc = Total Net Cost, Pav = Average of Total Net Costs.

local_preference_policy.policy_text: Local Preference Policy per Procurement Policy P000553.1: 5% discount (cap $50,000) for suppliers in Blacktown LGA; 2.5% discount (cap $25,000) for suppliers located in Western Sydney Councils.
local_preference_policy.application_note: Record eligible addresses in the evaluation spreadsheet (third tab).

pricing.value_for_money: Assessed on qualitative outcomes + price: quality and fitness for purpose vs whole-of-life costs vs risk.
pricing.interviews_and_presentation_criteria: Scores may be revised following interviews/presentations of shortlisted tenderers.

shortlisting: Clearly non-competitive tenders may be excluded with reasons recorded.
alternative_tenders: Alternative tenders will be evaluated per the RFT methodology.
tenderer_presentations: Interviews may be used for clarification only; no new information is accepted.
evaluation_report: Final results documented in a Council Report: method, number of tenders, rankings, recommendation, and rationale. Considered by the Tender Review Committee; endorsed and presented to Council.
contract_negotiations: Negotiations (post-tender) are fair, open, and must not materially change requirements. Outcomes documented.
debriefing_unsuccessful: Debriefing may be offered upon request. Only general comments on the tenderer’s own submission; no comparisons; no scoring details.
contract_management: The contract will be managed by <role/name>.
how_to_complete_tepp: 1) Title Page — Replace Tender Number, Name and Term.
2) 1. Aim — Update Tender Name and Term.
3) 2. Description of Requirement — Replace with contract description and main requirements.
4) 2.1 Desired Outcomes — Insert procurement outcomes (value for money, timely delivery, etc.).
5) 2.2 Purpose of RFT — Replace with reasons for this tender (or EOI/Quotation).
